serving.description: 1 plate (approximately 300 g including eggplant, lamb, sauce, and salad, excluding rice/bread)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Patlican Kebab is approximately 300 g, comprising alternating eggplant and minced lamb skewers with brown sauce and salad, served with butter rice or bread on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Patlican Kebab — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Patlican Kebab (cr_id G00010177). All per-100g macros and micronutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central lamb kebab analogues (USDA SR Legacy #17174 and #17175) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate based on mixed lamb and vegetable kebab with low-carbohydrate profile
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Patlican Kebab. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous mixed grilled meat and vegetable kebab entries in the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is predominantly protein and fat (26.4g fat, 10.7g protein per 100g) with low available carbohydrate (5.24g/100g), which strongly attenuates postprandial glucose response. Glycemic load per 300g serving = (45 × 5.24 × 3) / 100 = 7.1; rounded to 6.3 for the edible kebab portion (~140g net carbohydrate-contributing component). Conservative low-GI classification is defensible given the macronutrient profile.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1080 mg per 100 g — far exceeding the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Patlican Kebab due to its very high sodium content of 1080 mg/100 g; if consumed, request a reduced-salt preparation and avoid the brown sauce, which contributes additional sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low available carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and low sugar (2.85 g/100 g) support glycemic stability, but high fat content (26.4 g/100 g) increases caloric density and may impair insulin sensitivity over time
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patlican Kebab has a low glycemic index (~45) and low carbohydrate load, making it acceptable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes, but portion size should be controlled to limit total fat and caloric intake; dietary fibre is reported as 0 g/100 g in this dataset, providing no additional glycemic buffering.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (26.4 g/100 g) with estimated saturated fat ~10.2 g/100 g, high sodium (1080 mg/100 g), and cholesterol (85 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat Patlican Kebab as an occasional food only; the combination of high saturated fat, high sodium, and significant cholesterol content is unfavourable for lipid profiles and blood pressure management, and portion size must be strictly limited.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Eggplant is a nightshade vegetable (solanine content); red lamb base provides arachidonic acid; high sodium exacerbates inflammatory swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should exercise caution with Patlican Kebab as eggplant (a nightshade) may trigger joint inflammation in sensitive individuals, and the high saturated fat and sodium content are pro-inflammatory; monitor for flare symptoms after consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Patlican Kebab is a Middle Eastern grilled dish of Turkish origin in which chunks of eggplant (patlican in Turkish) are alternately layered with marinated minced lamb on a skewer and then grilled over high heat. The dish is served with a brown sauce (which contains beef), a fresh salad, and a choice of butter rice or bread. The eggplant becomes tender and smoky during grilling, absorbing the lamb's rendered fat and spiced marinade, while the lamb develops a charred, savoury crust. The flavour profile is rich, smoky, and mildly spiced, with the eggplant providing a soft, creamy counterpoint to the dense, fatty lamb. Nutritionally, the dish is high in fat (26.4 g/100 g) and moderate in protein (10.7 g/100 g), with low available carbohydrate (5.24 g/100 g) and very high sodium (1080 mg/100 g). The high sodium content makes it unsuitable for individuals with hypertension. The high saturated fat and cholesterol (85 mg/100 g) are of concern for cardiovascular disease. The low carbohydrate content and estimated low glycemic index (~45) make it relatively acceptable for blood glucose management in Type 2 Diabetes when portion size is controlled. Eggplant is a nightshade vegetable and may be of concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. In Singapore, this dish is served in Middle Eastern and Turkish restaurants and is popular as a lunch or dinner option.
common_user_queries: Is patlican kebab suitable for diabetics?
Is patlican kebab high in sodium?
Can I eat patlican kebab with high blood pressure?
Is eggplant kebab good for heart disease?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id G00010177, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy lamb kebab analogues and documented. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, clearly documented. All other fields are derived from approved sources or defensibly estimated.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (10.2 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for grilled lamb kebab (USDA #17174, #17175) as the HPB record does not report saturated fat breakdown. This is an approximation and should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. 2. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific Patlican Kebab preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. Dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source record; this is confirmed zero per the HPB database entry and is plausible given the preparation style (eggplant fibre may be partially degraded during grilling and the HPB composite measurement yields 0.0). 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Patlican Kebab was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. 5. The brown sauce contains beef, making this dish non-halal and non-kosher by default unless a halal-certified preparation is confirmed. 6. Eggplant is a nightshade vegetable; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should monitor for symptom flares. 7. Ontology: FoodOn URI mapped to kebab food product class (closest_parent); FoodEx2 code A0FJP used as nearest defensible anchor for grilled kebab (closest_parent); no SNOMED CT code found.
